Plaintiffs Motion To Issue Preliminary Injunctions And Lift Stay Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
David D. Bibiyan, Esq. (310.438.5555; david@tomorrowlaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Michael Kun, Esq. (mkun@ebglaw.com) for Defendants, Allegis.
Kimberli A. Williams, Esq. (kimberli.williams@morganlewis.com) for Defendant, Best Buy.
Eric Gitig, Esq. (213.689.0404; eric.gitig@jacksonlewis.com) for Defendant Adecco USA, Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANGELA DURAN'S Motion To Issue Preliminary Injunctions And Lift Stay Pending Arbitration: DENIED.

[SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS.]

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Oct-14-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BEST BUY WAREHOUSING LOGISTICS, LLC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER is Off calendar. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Continued To Nov-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Sara Ehsani-Nia, Esq; 310-438-5555, Bibiyan Law Group, P.C.; sara@tomorrowlaw.com; for Plaintiff Angela Duran.
No other appearances.

Court states to counsel that she did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling and that proper notice was not provided to opposing counsel. Court does not entertain argument on this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALLEGIS GLOBAL SOLUTIONS, INC., ALLEGIS GROUP HOLDINGS, INC.'S Joinder In Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Arbitration.

Granted. See accompanying tentative ruling.

Please see signed order on DEFENDANT ADECCO USA, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Arbitration.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Sara Ehsani-Nia, Esq; 310-438-5555, Bibiyan Law Group, P.C.; sara@tomorrowlaw.com; for Plaintiff Angela Duran.
No other appearances.

Court states to counsel that she did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling and that proper notice was not provided to opposing counsel. Court does not entertain argument on this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ADECCO USA, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Arbitration.

Defendants Addeco USA, Inc., Allegis Global Solutions, Inc., Allegis Group Holdings, Inc., and Best Buy Warehousing Logistics, LLC's motion for an order compelling arbitration of plaintiff's claims on an individual basis and dismissing without prejudice all of Plaintiff's claims is granted as unopposed. Defendants' motion for an order staying all proceedings on Plaintiff's representative claims for civil penalties under the California Private Attorneys' General Act of 2004 pending completion of the arbitration is granted. (9 U.S.C. Sec. 3 [stay mandatory under FAA]; see Franco v. Arakelian Enterprises, Inc. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 947, 966 ["Because the issues subject to litigation under the PAGA might overlap those that are subject to arbitration of [plaintiff's] individual claims, the trial court must order an appropriate stay of trial court proceedings. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 1281.4.) The stay's purpose is to preserve the status quo until the arbitration is resolved, preventing any continuing trial court proceedings from disrupting and rendering ineffective the arbitrator's jurisdiction to decide the issues that are subject to arbitration." (footnote and citation omitted)].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
